title: I Done 2

jokes.text: The word 'duh' comes to mind.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking what they'd say if he said he made a mistake about moving
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt, obvious response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Will-style response - economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Sound like that's what got caught in your throat.
jokes.setup: Carlton's 'early bird catches the worm' saying
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting Carlton's voice sounds like he swallowed a worm
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback about Carlton's voice/delivery

jokes.text: I mean, we got floor-to-ceiling walls and wall-to-wall floors.
jokes.setup: Will bragging about his fake apartment
jokes.punchline: Describing completely normal architectural features as special amenities
jokes.explanation: Classic Will absurdist humor - taking ordinary things and presenting them as luxuries. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I mean, uh, it's big in a real petite kind of way.
jokes.setup: Will caught contradicting himself about apartment size
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical oxymoron to cover his tracks
jokes.explanation: Good Will-style verbal gymnastics to avoid admitting the truth

jokes.text: Separation anxiety. You can't bear to have reminders of me in your new place.
jokes.setup: Will refuses to store Carlton's stuff
jokes.punchline: Carlton misinterprets it as emotional attachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - earnest psychology over-analysis. High character integration.

jokes.text: I got no-apartment anxiety.
jokes.setup: Carlton diagnosing Will with 'separation anxiety'
jokes.punchline: Will revealing the real problem with a clever word twist
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals the truth while maintaining the psychological framing

jokes.text: That's a question that haunts me every day.
jokes.setup: Will asking why Geoffrey is still at the house
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's existential response about his servitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey dry wit about his position - perfectly character-appropriate

jokes.text: Not selling the house? Yep.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking what would make Will happier than selling the house
jokes.punchline: Will's honest, simple response confirming Geoffrey's suggestion
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing Will's attachment to the house

jokes.text: Yeah, Willis would love it.
jokes.setup: Characters named Arnold and Willis looking at house
jokes.punchline: Reference to Diff'rent Strokes characters
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference joke. Relies on recognition more than wit.

jokes.text: Yeah, that is if he wouldn't mind living over a haunted Indian burial ground.
jokes.setup: Potential buyers (Arnold and Willis from Diff'rent Strokes) admiring the house
jokes.punchline: Will trying to scare them away with horror movie trope
jokes.explanation: Standard horror trope used to sabotage house showing

jokes.text: At night, you can hear the wailing of the dead.
jokes.setup: Continuing the haunted house bit
jokes.punchline: Escalating the spooky details
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the haunted house gag - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: What you talking about, Will?
jokes.setup: Arnold from Diff'rent Strokes appearing as house buyer
jokes.punchline: Using his famous catchphrase in response to Will
jokes.explanation: Pure nostalgia play with the famous Diff'rent Strokes catchphrase - high recognition factor

jokes.text: You know, those things were a lot funnier when you were still a little child.
jokes.setup: Arnold using his old catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Willis/older brother commenting on how the catchphrase has aged
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the aging of child star catchphrases - clever self-awareness

jokes.text: Yeah, well, Dad, your little stories were a lot more interesting back then too.
jokes.setup: Willis criticizing Arnold's catchphrase usage
jokes.punchline: Arnold firing back at Willis with family dynamic humor
jokes.explanation: Good sibling comeback playing on their established dynamic

jokes.text: Oh, believe me, Will. The fire went out a long time ago.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about the fire going out
jokes.punchline: Wife confirming the metaphorical meaning about their marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that lands the double meaning Will set up

jokes.text: Easy. He's cheap.
jokes.setup: George Jefferson asking how his wife can say they can't afford the house
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment of George's character
jokes.explanation: Direct character observation that plays on George Jefferson's known traits

jokes.text: One look at a bald-headed miser like you, they'll be crossing right back over.
jokes.setup: George's threat about people crossing the border for jobs
jokes.punchline: Will turning it around with an insult about George's appearance and personality
jokes.explanation: Escalating the insult game with George Jefferson

jokes.text: Memorial Day, it all comes off, you know what I'm talking about?
jokes.setup: Will telling George about the neighbors in bikinis
jokes.punchline: Implying nude sunbathing with suggestive delivery
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo to manipulate George's interest

jokes.text: Something about being in by Memorial Day and it all coming off.
jokes.setup: Will's earlier lie about Memorial Day activities
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil innocently repeating Will's fabricated selling point
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback where Uncle Phil unknowingly repeats Will's inappropriate sales pitch

jokes.text: I mean, Geoffrey only has two hands.
jokes.setup: Family discussing the impossibility of packing and moving in seven days
jokes.punchline: Vivian's matter-of-fact statement treating Geoffrey like their only resource
jokes.explanation: Perfect Vivian delivery showing how they take Geoffrey for granted

jokes.text: Geoffrey only has two hands.
jokes.setup: Family discusses impossible packing timeline
jokes.punchline: Vivian matter-of-factly states Geoffrey's physical limitation
jokes.explanation: Subtle humor about family's dependency on Geoffrey. Good character insight disguised as throwaway line.

jokes.text: Good one, Daddy.
jokes.setup: Philip suggesting the Plaza or Hilton hotels
jokes.punchline: Hilary thinking he made a joke when he was being practical
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary misunderstanding - she thinks practical suggestions are jokes

jokes.text: God built the whole universe in that much time. He had better credit.
jokes.setup: Carlton pointing out Will has seven days, like God creating the universe
jokes.punchline: Will's practical observation about God's financial advantages
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke combining biblical reference with modern economic reality - very quotable

jokes.text: It's because I'm black, ain't it?
jokes.setup: Will struggling to find an apartment despite having no job, references, or credit
jokes.punchline: Will deflecting from the obvious reasons to suggest racial discrimination
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as deflection - very Will-like avoidance of responsibility

jokes.text: Well, it's wall-to-wall doors and floor-to-ceiling floors.
jokes.setup: Family asking Carlton to describe Will's apartment
jokes.punchline: Carlton accidentally mixing up Will's earlier nonsensical description
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Will's earlier absurd description, with Carlton garbling it further

jokes.text: Because it's tented.
jokes.setup: Family wanting to see Will's apartment
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate excuse using pest control terminology
jokes.explanation: Decent desperate excuse - random but believable enough to buy time

jokes.text: It's a condo is what it is.
jokes.setup: Will finally admitting he doesn't have an apartment
jokes.punchline: Carlton still trying to help maintain the lie with increasingly desperate modifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton behavior - trying to fix things even when the truth is out

jokes.text: what you mean by verge? Don't push your luck.
jokes.setup: Phil calls Will someone 'on the verge' of realizing potential
jokes.punchline: Will questions the word choice, Phil shuts him down
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that lightens heavy scene. Shows their dynamic even in serious moments.

jokes.text: Yeah, look at you, boy. Six years, you couldn't fight the flood, could you?
jokes.setup: Carlton owning a Biggie Smalls CD despite his preppy nature
jokes.punchline: Will noting how Carlton has been influenced by urban culture over the years
jokes.explanation: Great observation about the show's six-year character development - Will's influence on Carlton

jokes.text: Well, you know, I'd like to think of myself as with it.
jokes.setup: Carlton surprised Phil owns a Biggie Smalls CD
jokes.punchline: Phil's awkward attempt to sound hip
jokes.explanation: Standard 'dad trying to be cool' humor - works because it's so Phil

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I know I don't own two What's New Pussycats?
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton sorting through CDs
jokes.punchline: Will realizing Carlton owns the cheesy Tom Jones album
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the contrast between Will and Carlton's music tastes

jokes.text: That just doesn't sound right coming from me, does it?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to say 'I've got your back, W' like Will does
jokes.punchline: Carlton's self-aware acknowledgment that he can't pull off Will's street talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton self-awareness - knows his limitations with street vernacular

jokes.text: Carlton performing 'It's Not Unusual' by Tom Jones
jokes.setup: Will asking Carlton to perform one last time
jokes.punchline: Carlton's enthusiastic, over-the-top performance of his signature song
jokes.explanation: Iconic Carlton moment - his signature dance/song that became a cultural phenomenon

jokes.text: Yes! Ha-ha-ha! I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Philip telling Geoffrey he's officially off duty
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's immediate celebration followed by embarrassed apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - his true feelings about servitude briefly exposed

jokes.text: I'm getting lightheaded.
jokes.setup: Philip giving Geoffrey both first-class upgrade AND spring bonus
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's understated reaction to unprecedented generosity
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey understatement in response to unusual kindness from the family

jokes.text: Just kidding.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking Will to help with his bag
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey revealing it was a joke - he can handle his own luggage
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection from Geoffrey - brief role reversal moment

jokes.text: Hey, who turned out the lights?
jokes.setup: Jazz arrives to help Will move after everyone has left
jokes.punchline: He arrives to a dark, empty house
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy ending. Jazz's timing is characteristically off, providing closure.

jokes.text: I am definitely gonna miss you, C.
jokes.setup: Will alone in the empty house talking to Carlton's abandoned underwear
jokes.punchline: Will expressing genuine emotion to an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment - comedy and pathos combined as Will talks to Carlton's underwear

tmdb_overview: The Banks move out. The Jeffersons are about to move in. And Will turns out the lights.
